---

# UC-18.1.9 · Cisco ACI Contract Permit-vs-Deny Ratio Trending: actrlRuleHit5min Per-Rule Counter Analysis, Implicit-Deny Burst Detection, EPG-Pair Policy Coverage Gaps, Filter Entry Effectiveness Scoring, acllog Flow Forensics

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Security, Performance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We tally how often each tenant agreement welcomes traffic versus quietly refuses it—like a doorkeeper's scorecard—so quiet misconfigurations surface as shifting numbers before workloads stall.*

---

## Description

Tracks the ratio of explicitly permitted traffic to implicitly or explicitly denied traffic per Cisco ACI contract, subject, and filter by analysing **actrlRuleHit5min** five-minute hardware counters compiled from **actrlRule** entries on each leaf TCAM—classifying each compiled rule as **explicit_permit** (matching a **vzFilter**/**vzEntry** permit), **explicit_deny** (matching a taboo contract or deny filter), or **implicit_deny** (the auto-generated catch-all rule that drops traffic for which no contract exists between the communicating **fvAEPg** pair)—then correlating counter trends with **acllogPermitL3Pkt**/**acllogDropL3Pkt** per-flow forensics and syslog faults **F1394**, **F0467**, **F2301**, **F0952** to distinguish genuine policy gaps from operational noise during contract migrations, filter resequencing, **vzAny** enablement, and **prefGroupMemb** toggling.

## Value

Silent contract misconfigurations are the most dangerous class of ACI policy failure because east-west production traffic drops at implicit deny rules without generating the dramatic syslog storms that traditional monitoring catches—applications time out, databases report connection resets, and load balancers mark backends unhealthy while fabric dashboards show green health scores because the fabric itself is operating exactly as policy dictates. Ratio trending catches this failure mode: when a permit/deny ratio stable at 0.95 for weeks drops to 0.6 after a midnight change window, the contract subject lost coverage—either a filter entry was deleted, a port range narrowed below actual usage, or an EPG moved to a contract whose subjects lack the needed protocol match. Catching the ratio shift within fifteen minutes means restoring the missing filter before the NOC escalates application-layer symptoms.

## Implementation

Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**) for syslog normalization; deploy HTTPS REST collectors polling **actrlRuleHit5min**, **actrlRule**, **vzBrCP** (with full subtree), **acllogPermitL3Pkt**, **acllogDropL3Pkt**, and **faultInst** into `index=cisco_aci`. Enable **statsEnabled=true** on target contract subjects and ACL logging per-subject for flow-level forensics. Schedule **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** every five minutes with fifteen-minute alert throttle per tenant + contract pair.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** merging **`cisco:aci:contract_stats`**, **`cisco:aci:acl_log`**, **`cisco:aci:contract_scope`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`** plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_contract_ratio.json`** with **`tok_tenant`**, **`tok_contract`**, and **`tok_epg_pair`** drill tokens.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco **APIC** clusters on **6.0(x)**, **6.1(x)**, or **6.2(x)** with HTTPS reachability from Splunk collectors on 443/tcp; dedicate a **read-only** automation account isolated from interactive admins because overlapping **aaaRefresh.json** rotations across shared identities collapse concurrent collectors.
- Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) wherever APIC syslog terminates; the TA normalises faults into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog` but does not poll REST contract-statistics endpoints—supplement with **rest_ta** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or Python modular inputs authenticated via `POST /api/aaaLogin.json`.
- Set **statsEnabled=true** on each target **vzSubj** via REST `POST /api/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}/brc-{contract}.json` with `{"vzBrCP":{"attributes":{"statsEnabled":"true"}}}`. Without this flag **actrlRuleHit5min** counters remain zero even with active dataplane traffic—the single most common missed prerequisite.
- Enable **ACL logging** per vzSubj at log or logAndDeny level so **acllogPermitL3Pkt** and **acllogDropL3Pkt** emit per-flow five-tuple records (**sIp**, **dIp**, **sProt**, **dProt**, **prot**); budget capacity because large fabrics generate 10,000–100,000 flow events per five-minute window per leaf pair.
- APIC automation accounts need read access to **/api/class/actrlRuleHit5min.json**, **/api/class/actrlRule.json**, **/api/class/vzBrCP.json**, **/api/class/acllogPermitL3Pkt.json**, **/api/class/acllogDropL3Pkt.json**.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **Contract statistics** — HTTPS GET against **/api/class/actrlRuleHit5min.json?rsp-subtree-include=stats** at 60–180 s cadence; each response carries per-**actrlRule** counters (**ingrPkts**, **egrPkts**, **ingrBytes**, **egrBytes**) with DN paths embedding tn-{tenant}, brc-{contract}, subj-{subject}, flt-{filter}—normalise into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_stats` extracting **actEntry**, **fltId**, **scopeId**, **sClass**, **dClass**.
(2) **ACL flow logs** — Poll **/api/class/acllogPermitL3Pkt.json** and **/api/class/acllogDropL3Pkt.json** into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:acl_log` preserving five-tuple fields plus the **actrlRule** DN linking each flow to its contract—provides per-flow forensics supplementing aggregate counters.
(3) **Contract config snapshots** — Poll **/api/class/vzBrCP.json?rsp-subtree=full** at 300–900 s into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_scope`; key fields: vzBrCP.name, **vzSubj.consMatchT**/provMatchT, vzFilter.name, **vzEntry.dFromPort**/dToPort/prot/stateful, and statsEnabled so Splunk warns when statistics are disabled.
(4) **Syslog faults** — Filter for **F1394** (contract-deny threshold), **F0467** (config-mismatch), **F2301** (EPG binding failure), **F0952** (TCAM programming failure), plus mnemonic ACLLOG-5-ACLLOG_PKTLOG.
(5) **Fault instances** — Poll **/api/class/faultInst.json** filtered by contract codes into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault` capturing **severity**, **lifecycle**, timestamps, and full DN.
(6) **Volume** — actrlRuleHit5min generates ~2–4 KB/poll/compiled-rule; 500 rules at 120 s ≈ 1,500–3,000 events per five-minute window. ACL flow logs scale with traffic diversity—budget 5–20× contract-stats volume.

### Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert
Schedule **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** at cron `*/5 * * * *` over `earliest=-24h@m`; throttle tenant_tag + contract_tag for 15 minutes.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
  sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:acl_log","cisco:aci:contract_scope")
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952|ACLLOG|implicitRule)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","stats",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:acl_log","flow",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_scope","scope",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",1=1,"misc")
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn,"")))
| rex field=dn_lc "tn-(?<tenant_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "brc-(?<contract_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "subj-(?<subject_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "flt-(?<filter_tag>[^/]+)"
| eval act_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(actEntry,action,rule_action,"")))
| eval rule_class=case(
    match(act_raw,"^permit"), "explicit_permit",
    match(dn_lc,"implicitrule") AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "implicit_deny",
    match(act_raw,"^deny"), "explicit_deny",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^permit"), "flow_permit",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "flow_deny",
    lane IN ("sf","fi"), "fault_signal",
    1=1, "unclassified")
| eval pkts_permit=case(
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit"), tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,permit_count,if(lane=="flow","1","0"))),
    1=1, 0)
| eval pkts_deny=case(
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny"), tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,ingrPkts,if(lane=="flow","1","0"))),
    1=1, 0)
| eval bytes_total=tonumber(coalesce(ingrBytes,ingress_bytes,0))+tonumber(coalesce(egrBytes,egress_bytes,0))
| eval src_pctag=trim(coalesce(sClass,src_class_id,sPcTag,""))
| eval dst_pctag=trim(coalesce(dClass,dst_class_id,dPcTag,""))
| eval fault_code=if(lane IN ("sf","fi"),trim(coalesce(fault_code,code,"")),"")
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats sum(pkts_permit) as tot_permit sum(pkts_deny) as tot_deny max(bytes_total) as peak_bytes values(rule_class) as rule_classes values(filter_tag) as filter_tags values(fault_code) as fault_codes dc(coalesce(fltId,filter_id)) as unique_filters latest(src_pctag) as s_pctag latest(dst_pctag) as d_pctag count as evt_count by win15 tenant_tag contract_tag subject_tag
| eval ratio=round(if((tot_permit+tot_deny)==0,null(),tot_permit/(tot_permit+tot_deny)),4)
| eval implicit_dom=if(mvfind(rule_classes,"implicit_deny")>=0 AND tot_deny>tot_permit,"YES","no")
| eval contract_path=tenant_tag."/".contract_tag."/".subject_tag
| eval risk_deny_flood=if(tot_deny>500 AND ratio<0.5,1,0)
| eval risk_implicit_storm=if(implicit_dom=="YES" AND tot_deny>1000,1,0)
| eval risk_ratio_collapse=if(ratio<0.3 AND (tot_permit+tot_deny)>100,1,0)
| eval risk_zero_permit=if(tot_permit==0 AND tot_deny>0,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(mvcount(fault_codes)>0 AND match(mvjoin(fault_codes,","),"F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952"),1,0)
| where risk_deny_flood=1 OR risk_implicit_storm=1 OR risk_ratio_collapse=1 OR risk_zero_permit=1 OR risk_fault=1
| sort -tot_deny -evt_count
| head 250
| table win15 tenant_tag contract_path ratio tot_permit tot_deny implicit_dom filter_tags s_pctag d_pctag fault_codes risk_deny_flood risk_implicit_storm risk_ratio_collapse peak_bytes evt_count
```

**Pipeline walkthrough** — Five sourcetypes merge into lanes: **stats** = aggregate hardware counters, **flow** = per-flow forensics, **scope** = config baseline, **sf**/**fi** = fault signals. Rule classification separates **explicit_permit** (vzFilter match), **implicit_deny** (catch-all where no contract exists—primary signal for policy gaps), and **explicit_deny** (vzTaboo blocking). The distinction matters operationally: implicit deny means missing policy; explicit deny means intentional blocking.

**Ratio = tot_permit / (tot_permit + tot_deny)** — 1.0 = all traffic permitted; 0.0 = all denied. `ratio < 0.5` flags contracts where more traffic is denied than permitted. `ratio < 0.3` triggers **risk_ratio_collapse** for critical investigation. The `tot_deny > 500` floor filters test-tenant noise. Tune per tenant criticality—shared-services may tolerate 0.7 while production should alert at 0.9. **Implicit deny dominance** (implicit_dom = YES) means denials come from the catch-all rather than a deny filter—EPG pairs communicating with no contract at all.

### Step 3 — Validate against APIC and leaf hardware
(1) Navigate **Tenants → {tenant} → Application Profiles → {AP} → EPGs → {EPG} → Contracts → Statistics**; 5min counters should match Splunk tot_permit/tot_deny within one poll lag. Zero counters despite traffic confirm statsEnabled=false.
(2) On a leaf: `show zoning-rule scope <scopeId>` displays compiled **actrlRule** entries with **sClass**, **dClass**, **fltId**, **action**—cross-check against Splunk's rule_class. Run `show system internal policy-mgr stats` to confirm counters increment; zeroes despite traffic imply **F0952**.
(3) Use **Fabric → Troubleshooting → Contract Checker** with source/destination EPG pair to verify explicit contract versus implicit deny—compare with Splunk's implicit_dom flag.
(4) When risk_ratio_collapse fires, drill into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:acl_log rule_class=flow_deny` to extract denied five-tuples; compare against **vzEntry** ranges—if the denied port falls outside all dFromPort/dToPort ranges, the filter needs a new entry.
(5) Diff latest contract_scope snapshot against nightly baseline (`| outputlookup aci_contract_baseline.csv`) to detect filter deletions or port-range narrowing.

### Step 4 — Operationalise and alert routing
(1) **Dashboard** — Top row: ratio timechart overlaid with tot_deny per contract_path. Second row: tenant × contract heatmap by ratio (green ≥0.9, amber 0.5–0.89, red <0.5). Third row: implicit_dom=YES contracts. Fourth row: acllog five-tuple drilldown.
(2) **Routing** — **risk_ratio_collapse** to ACI fabric team. **risk_implicit_storm** to application architecture (missing contracts need app-tier awareness). **risk_fault** with F0952 to fabric infrastructure (TCAM exhaustion affects all tenants on the leaf).
(3) **Suppression** — Maintain **contract_maint_windows.csv** keyed by tenant_tag + contract_tag with window_start/window_end from change management; join and suppress during documented migrations.
(4) **Runbook** — Contracts in the common tenant affect all VRFs and need fabric-admin approval. Application-tenant contracts route to app-team on-call. Include deep links to APIC Contract Checker and Faults pages.
(5) **Visualization** — Colour ratio tiles using Dashboard Studio conditional formatting. Single-value tile showing active F1394 faults beside the ratio timechart. Alert cron: `*/5 * * * *` earliest=-1h@m for paging; earliest=-24h@m for trending. Triggered alert with contract_path suppression for 15 minutes.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **Zero actrlRuleHit5min events despite traffic** — Verify statsEnabled=true on the vzSubj via REST GET. If recently enabled, counters populate within ~5 minutes. Also check Statistics collection policy at Fabric → Fabric Policies → Monitoring → Statistics.

- **All ratios show 1.0** — ACL logging disabled (only permits visible) or VRF uses **prefGroupMemb=include** making all EPG communication implicitly permitted. Verify with `show zoning-rule` on a leaf—no implicit deny rule means preferred-group mode where deny counters are structurally absent.

- **Ratio collapsed but no application impact** — Denied traffic may be scanner noise or broadcast. Drill into acl_log denied flows: ICMP echo, NetBIOS (137–139/UDP), mDNS (5353/UDP), SSDP (1900/UDP) are commonly benign.

- **F0952 TCAM programming failures** — Leaf exhausted policy TCAM. Run `vsh_lc -c "show platform internal hal l2 phase4 policy-stats"` to check utilisation. Consolidate overlapping filters—unprogrammed rules cause traffic to fall through to implicit deny.

- **Stale data after aaaRefresh failure** — Collector's aaaRefresh returns 401/403 and subsequent polls fail silently—Splunk serves stale counters masking ratio changes. Check splunkd.log for aaaRefresh errors; alert on consecutive failed polls.

- **Duplicate counters from multiple collectors** — Two collectors polling the same APIC double-count volumes; ratio stays correct but volume thresholds trigger prematurely. Stamp each collector with a **collector_id** and deduplicate before stats.

- **pcTag changes after EPG migration** — APIC reallocates pcTags when EPGs migrate between bridge domains; historical data keyed by old pcTags becomes orphaned. Maintain a **pcTag_history.csv** lookup mapping EPG DNs to historical pcTags.

- **Alert fires on every contract simultaneously** — Indicates systemic issue rather than per-contract misconfiguration. Check for fabric-wide TCAM exhaustion, APIC cluster partition, or NTP clock skew causing indextime misalignment.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
  sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:acl_log","cisco:aci:contract_scope")
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952|ACLLOG|implicitRule)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","stats",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:acl_log","flow",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_scope","scope",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",1=1,"misc")
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn,"")))
| rex field=dn_lc "tn-(?<tenant_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "brc-(?<contract_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "subj-(?<subject_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "flt-(?<filter_tag>[^/]+)"
| eval act_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(actEntry,action,rule_action,"")))
| eval rule_class=case(
    match(act_raw,"^permit"), "explicit_permit",
    match(dn_lc,"implicitrule") AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "implicit_deny",
    match(act_raw,"^deny"), "explicit_deny",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^permit"), "flow_permit",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "flow_deny",
    lane IN ("sf","fi"), "fault_signal",
    1=1, "unclassified")
| eval pkts_permit=case(
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit"), tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,permit_count,if(lane=="flow","1","0"))),
    1=1, 0)
| eval pkts_deny=case(
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny"), tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,ingrPkts,if(lane=="flow","1","0"))),
    1=1, 0)
| eval bytes_total=tonumber(coalesce(ingrBytes,ingress_bytes,0))+tonumber(coalesce(egrBytes,egress_bytes,0))
| eval src_pctag=trim(coalesce(sClass,src_class_id,sPcTag,""))
| eval dst_pctag=trim(coalesce(dClass,dst_class_id,dPcTag,""))
| eval fault_code=if(lane IN ("sf","fi"),trim(coalesce(fault_code,code,"")),"")
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats sum(pkts_permit) as tot_permit sum(pkts_deny) as tot_deny max(bytes_total) as peak_bytes values(rule_class) as rule_classes values(filter_tag) as filter_tags values(fault_code) as fault_codes dc(coalesce(fltId,filter_id)) as unique_filters latest(src_pctag) as s_pctag latest(dst_pctag) as d_pctag count as evt_count by win15 tenant_tag contract_tag subject_tag
| eval ratio=round(if((tot_permit+tot_deny)==0,null(),tot_permit/(tot_permit+tot_deny)),4)
| eval implicit_dom=if(mvfind(rule_classes,"implicit_deny")>=0 AND tot_deny>tot_permit,"YES","no")
| eval contract_path=tenant_tag."/".contract_tag."/".subject_tag
| eval risk_deny_flood=if(tot_deny>500 AND ratio<0.5,1,0)
| eval risk_implicit_storm=if(implicit_dom=="YES" AND tot_deny>1000,1,0)
| eval risk_ratio_collapse=if(ratio<0.3 AND (tot_permit+tot_deny)>100,1,0)
| eval risk_zero_permit=if(tot_permit==0 AND tot_deny>0,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(mvcount(fault_codes)>0 AND match(mvjoin(fault_codes,","),"F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952"),1,0)
| where risk_deny_flood=1 OR risk_implicit_storm=1 OR risk_ratio_collapse=1 OR risk_zero_permit=1 OR risk_fault=1
| sort -tot_deny -evt_count
| head 250
| table win15 tenant_tag contract_path ratio tot_permit tot_deny implicit_dom filter_tags s_pctag d_pctag fault_codes risk_deny_flood risk_implicit_storm risk_ratio_collapse peak_bytes evt_count
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart — **ratio** overlaid with **tot_deny** stacked bars per **contract_path**, colour-coded by **implicit_dom** flag. (2) Heatmap — **tenant_tag** × **contract_tag** cells shaded by **ratio** (green ≥0.9, amber 0.5–0.89, red <0.5). (3) Single-value tiles — contracts currently below 0.5 ratio, total implicit_deny events in last hour, active **F1394** faults. (4) Table — worst contract_path entries ranked by tot_deny with filter_tags, s_pctag, d_pctag for EPG-pair identification. (5) Drilldown — clicking any contract_path opens acllog flow-level detail showing denied five-tuples to identify which application traffic the missing filter should cover.

## Known False Positives

**contract_migration_window** — Operators migrating EPGs between contracts during application-tier restructuring cause transient deny spikes while new permit rules compile across leaf TCAM—the ratio dip reflects compilation lag rather than policy failure; suppress by correlating **aaaModLR** entries showing **vzBrCP** or **vzConsCtrct** edits and stamping maintenance-window tags until actrlRule entries stabilise.

**filter_entry_resequence** — Security teams reordering **vzEntry** priority within filters briefly invalidate compiled **actrlRule** entries while replacement hardware rules propagate—ratio oscillations during the window (typically 30–90 seconds per leaf) do not indicate traffic loss if ingrPkts on newly compiled rules begins incrementing within two poll cycles.

**epg_vzany_enablement** — Enabling **vzAny** provider contracts on a VRF broadens implicit scope from per-EPG to per-VRF evaluation, causing a one-time surge in implicit deny visibility as previously unseen EPG-pair combinations report statistics through the vzAny aggregation path—baseline ratios shift permanently and must be recalibrated within twenty-four hours.

**taboo_contract_addition** — Adding **vzTaboo** contracts generates new explicit deny rules overriding normal permits for blacklisted traffic; deny counters spike because the taboo represents intentional security hardening—distinguish from misconfiguration by correlating vzTaboo creation timestamps in aaaModLR and confirming the fltId matches the taboo filter chain.

**preferred_group_toggle** — Toggling **prefGroupMemb=include** on EPGs switches communication from deny-by-default to permit-by-default among group members; the ratio inverts dramatically—verify against fvEPg attribute change records rather than assuming permit floods indicate leaked security posture.

**consumer_provider_swap_window** — Operators deleting a **vzConsCtrct** binding and re-adding as **vzProvCtrct** to adjust directionality generate transient implicit deny hits during the gap—correlate paired delete-then-create operations on the same fvAEPg within a five-minute window before paging.

**pcTag_reallocation_event** — APIC periodically reallocates **policy control tags** during VRF-scale events or fabric-partition recovery; actrlRule entries referencing old pcTags stop counting while replacements compile—ratio collapses for one or two poll cycles but recovers autonomously.

## References

- [Cisco APIC Basic Configuration Guide — Contracts and Filters (Release 6.0(x))](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/aci/apic/6x/configuration/b-cisco-apic-basic-configuration-guide-60x/b-cisco-apic-basic-configuration-guide-60x_chapter_010000.html)
- [Cisco APIC REST API Configuration Guide (Release 6.0(x))](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/aci/apic/6x/rest-cfg/cisco-apic-rest-api-configuration-guide-60x.html)
- [Cisco APIC MIM Reference — actrlRuleHit5min Class](https://developer.cisco.com/docs/apic-mim-ref/)
- [Cisco ACI Contract and Subject Design White Paper](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/solutions/collateral/data-center-virtualization/application-centric-infrastructure/white-paper-c11-743951.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (Splunkbase 4022)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)
- [Splunk REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1546)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)
- [Cisco APIC Troubleshooting Guide — Contract and Policy (Release 6.0(x))](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/aci/apic/6x/troubleshooting-guide/cisco-apic-troubleshooting-guide-60x.html)
